mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering immediate risk-off positioning in equity markets and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points within a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the t-statistic on forward returns exceeds -1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and growth in real personal savings rates indicates robust aggregate demand supported by supply capacity, driving earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where the federal funds rate exceeds the inflation rate, real borrowing costs remain elevated, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 55% of high real rate observations, or the coefficient on real rates in a rolling regression remains positive and significant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and construction sector momentum, which leads equity markets to price in stronger near-term economic activity and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or forward returns show no directional bias relative to the baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index breaks above 105.0, it confirms full capacity utilization and strong manufacturing demand, prompting upward earnings revisions that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 60% of instances where INDPRO exceeds the threshold, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strong nonfarm payroll growth and a rising federal funds rate indicate an overheating labor market meeting tightening monetary policy, which historically precedes equity drawdowns and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to produce a statistically significant negative coefficient in a rolling regression, or forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: During disinflationary growth episodes where real GDP expands while CPI growth decelerates, corporate margins expand without monetary tightening, directly supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 60% of soft landing observations, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth falls below 2.0% annualized.
